title: AI Is Already Operating at Scale in SAP Customer Support
intent: Show an operational example of AI moving beyond demos into high-volume support workflows.

source.title: Transforming Customer Support With Artificial Intelligence
source.methodology: SAP customer support operational metrics described by SAP leadership.

fact.primary_stat: SAP said its AI support scenarios were called more than 1 million times by customers on an average day.
supporting_stats: SAP reported more than 35,000 end users use its AI capabilities per month.
SAP said it had developed more than 50 AI-driven use cases in customer support.

business_relevance: Customer support is proving to be one of the clearest enterprise AI operating zones.
Search, routing, summarization, clustering, and proactive recommendations are now observable at production scale.
dzmitryi_commentary: This is the closest fit to my profile. Support is where AI becomes concrete because the inputs are repetitive but context-heavy: tickets, KBs, logs, routing signals, and historical fixes. For SAP AMS teams, the lesson is to start where operational memory already exists and where cycle time, recurrence, and handover quality can be measured.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Show an operational example of AI moving beyond demos into high-volume support workflows.

attribution.preferred_citation: Dzmitryi Kharlanau. “AI Is Already Operating at Scale in SAP Customer Support” (dataset bytes). CC BY-NC 4.0. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18862098. https://dkharlanau.github.io/datasets/ai-business-signals/aibs-005.json
